test.summary: typecheck 0; full suite 1732; ux-qa-policy + validate threshold tests committed

adversarialReview.finding: installed-set staleness heals at apm install (S7 scope)

uxQa.target: kanban-loop board UI (sandboxed instance; not the S5 diff — see ux-qa/findings.md scope note)

findings.what: every interactive control is below the 44px touch comfort target on the phone-first board: list config gear 28x28, all card actions (Advance/Move/Watch/Open/Run/Discuss) 31px tall; the 24x24 WCAG floor is met so this is a comfort/HIG failure, escalated per the checklist's touch-surface clause
findings.fix: at @media (max-width:600px) set .card .btns .btn { min-height:44px } and .list .lname .gear { width:44px; height:44px } (keep the 28px glyph, grow the hit area with padding)

findings.what: modal sheets never take focus: 12 consecutive Tabs from the open card-detail sheet all land on controls on the board BEHIND the overlay (sheet.contains(activeElement)===false for all 12), so a keyboard user can never reach Close / Abandon / Delete and the focus ring is drawn on visually obscured controls
findings.where: card-detail sheet (and the New-card sheet) — the shared Sheet component in ui/main.tsx
findings.fix: role=dialog + aria-modal=true; focus the sheet container (tabIndex=-1) or Close on mount; cycle Tab/Shift+Tab within the sheet; restore focus to the invoker on close; mark the board inert while a sheet is open

findings.what: five text styles fail 4.5:1 against their composited backgrounds: .chip.goal 2.79:1, .list.codex .lname-text 3.10:1, .list .lkind .cdx 3.10:1, .chip.waiting 3.39:1, .card .state-callout.waiting 3.39:1 — all trace to --brass #b4862a and --warn #b07215 used as text on --paper #fbf8f1 / --warn-soft #f6ecd0; none qualify as large text
findings.where: board — goalMode/waiting chips, adversarial list titles + phase line, the waiting callout
findings.fix: darken the text tokens: #8a6410 measures 4.56:1 on --warn-soft and 5.06:1 on --paper (#7d5a0e for margin); add --brass-ink/--warn-ink for text usages, keep --brass for the left-edge accent

findings.what: mobile type is under the 16px floor — body 14px, card title 13px, chips 10px — and every New-card control is 13.5px (#nc-title, #nc-desc, #nc-project, #nc-kind), which makes iOS Safari auto-zoom the page on focus
findings.fix: at the mobile breakpoint set body { font-size:16px } and input, select, textarea { font-size:16px } (this is the line that suppresses the iOS zoom); raise the card title to >=16px and chips to >=12px

findings.what: an empty list renders a bare muted lowercase word 'empty' at the top of an otherwise blank ~1000px column — no heading, no explanation of what belongs there, no next-step CTA
findings.where: Adversarial Review and Adversarial Test columns — div.lempty, ui/main.tsx:1496
findings.fix: replace with an empty-state block: heading ('No cards'), one sentence naming how cards arrive here (derivable from the list's phase/validNext), and an inline 'New card' CTA on manual lists

findings.what: the New-card validation message is good prose and passes contrast (6.04:1) but renders ONLY as a generic .banner at the bottom of the sheet, 335px below the Title field it is about (banner y=629, title y=253); no aria-invalid on the fields, no role=alert/aria-live on the banner, and on 390px it sits below the fold so the press appears to do nothing
findings.where: New-card sheet — submit with Title and Description both empty
findings.fix: render the message inline under the Title/Description fields, set aria-invalid + aria-describedby on them, add role=alert to the banner; keep the wording

findings.what: on a manual list with exactly one valid next list (Backlog -> todo), Advance and Move sit side by side and do the same thing (Move short-circuits to a direct PATCH, ui/main.tsx:1508-1516; verified live: Backlog 1->0, To Do 1->2 with no picker), and neither label names the destination
findings.where: card front on Backlog / any single-validNext manual list
findings.fix: label the destination ('Advance -> To Do') and hide Move when validNext.length === 1; keep Move ('Move...') only where a choice exists

findings.what: after Advance the notice reads 'Moved to todo' — the internal list id — while every other surface (column header, Move picker, activity log) calls the same list 'To Do'
findings.where: notice banner after Advance — onStart, ui/main.tsx:1381
findings.fix: resolve the id to the list title before rendering: board.lists.find(l => l.id === res.advanced)?.title

findings.what: at 390px the topbar wraps: the brand becomes 'Kanban / Loop', the count chip '11 / CARDS', and the primary CTA wraps to two lines inside its own button ('New / card')
findings.fix: white-space: nowrap on .topbar .btn.primary and the count chip; drop the 'WORKFLOW BOARD' eyebrow and tighten topbar padding at the mobile breakpoint

findings.what: on a card with no run, the artifact table shows seven rows (PLAN, BRIEF, SESSIONS, GATE MARKERS, EVIDENCE INDEX, VIDEO, LOGS) each with a bare em dash and no explanation of when they fill — while the adjacent DECISION LOG section does explain ('No runs recorded yet.'), so the screen is inconsistent with itself
findings.where: card-detail sheet for a Backlog card
findings.fix: mirror the DECISION LOG treatment: one line of group context ('No artifacts yet - these fill in as the card moves through Plan, Implement and Walkthrough'), keeping per-row dashes only once a run exists

findings.what: 13 lists in a horizontal scroller at 390px with no pagination dots, list switcher or position indicator; reaching Needs attention from Backlog takes ~12 swipes. Conventional mobile-kanban behaviour (hence a note), but the convention normally ships with a jump affordance
findings.fix: optional - add a compact list switcher (a select of the 13 lists that scrolls the chosen column into view) or pagination dots to the mobile topbar
